---
title: "About"
pagetitle: "About — DigiPols"
toc: true
toc-location: right
---

```{=html}
<div class="page-header">
  <h1 style="font-size: clamp(2.4rem, 5vw, 3.6rem); line-height: 1.02; letter-spacing: -0.03em; font-weight: 500; max-width: 22ch; margin: 0;">An inter-departmental research lab at the Cyprus University of Technology.</h1>
  <p class="lede">DigiPols pursues an interdisciplinary approach that brings together social scientists and computer scientists conducting complementary research on the intersection of digital technologies and politics.</p>
</div>
```

## Mission

DigiPols provides a research environment for pursuing cutting-edge, theory-driven empirical research for advancing the study of how digital technologies are impacting our institutions of governance — and how politics shapes the development of these technologies.

The lab's researchers are brought together in the **DigiPols Forum**, which meets regularly to exchange ideas, discuss best practices and offer strategic input to the four research clusters.

## Host institution

DigiPols is hosted by the Department of Communication and Internet Studies and the Department of Public Communication at the [Cyprus University of Technology](https://www.cut.ac.cy/), Limassol, Cyprus.

## Contact

- Email: [digipols@cut.ac.cy](mailto:digipols@cut.ac.cy)
- Address: Cyprus University of Technology, 30 Archbishop Kyprianos Str., 3036 Limassol, Cyprus
- GitHub: [github.com/digipols](https://github.com/)

## Funding

Work at DigiPols has been supported by Horizon Europe, the European Parliament, INTERREG, the Cyprus Research and Innovation Foundation and the Recovery and Resilience Plan of the Republic of Cyprus.
